original_hypothesis.title: Guardian quorum can be tricked into approving one transition while final submitter chooses different proof payload (proof.data not bound to approvals)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GuardianProver approval hash commits only to (meta, tran), not to the TierProof contents. The last guardian to reach threshold supplies _proof.data that gets forwarded into TaikoL1.proveBlock. If any verifier logic for guardian tier ever interprets proof.data (now or in future upgrades), or if top-tier RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND signaling were enabled at guardian tier in TaikoL1/LibProving patterns, a malicious final guardian could change operational semantics at submission time without other guardians explicitly approving that exact payload.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if current GuardianVerifier ignores proof content, this is a governance/ops footgun that can become exploitable after upgrades or configuration changes. It also weakens auditability of what guardians actually agreed to sign off on.
deployment_assumptions: Guardian tier verifier or TaikoL1 proving path uses/starts using _proof.data for any decision (now or via upgrade)
At least minGuardians honest guardians approve meta+tran without verifying the exact proof bytes used in the final submission
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment evidence that proof.data is used for guardian tier today; GuardianVerifier currently ignores proof payload.

proof_of_signal.pattern: hash = keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)); approved_ = approve(_meta.id, hash); ... proveBlock(_meta.id, abi.encode(_meta, _tran, _proof));

preconditions: Malicious guardian can be the last signer to reach quorum
Other guardians approve based on meta+tran only (as enforced)
Guardian tier proof bytes can influence acceptance or bond behavior (needs validation)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Quorum reached with consistent meta+tran but on-chain submission includes unexpected proof bytes.

operational_questions: Do guardian processes verify/record the exact bytes submitted as _proof?
Is proof.data currently ignored for guardian tier everywhere (including in TaikoL1/LibProving special paths)?
Could future upgrades introduce proof.data semantics for guardian tier?
validation_requirements: Review GuardianVerifier and tier_provider mappings for guardian tier
Diff guardian proof payloads used in submissions vs what signers intended off-chain

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Coordinate to collect guardian approvals for a given meta+tran.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: As the last guardian, submit approve() with altered _proof.data.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: If proof.data has semantics, realize the altered outcome (e.g., bypass checks, trigger special behavior).

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Unexpected proof acceptance behavior if proof.data gains meaning
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium; requires contract change to include proof in hash

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: When quorum is about to be reached

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Mismatch between off-chain guardian attestation logs and on-chain calldata
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Same guardian frequently acting as final submitter

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
